Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions, To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GRIFFIN & SULLIVAN, KEVIN SULLIVAN'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions, To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions

Papazian v. Sullivan, et al., No. CGC-17-558695. At the time the requests for admission were served, the defendants' sole attorneys of record were the law firm of Dimitriou & Associates. The Requests for Admission were signed by attorney Daniel White who was of a separate law firm, the Law Office of Daniel P. White. About a week later, an Association of Attorneys was filed, associating into the case, the Law Office of Daniel P. White. Plaintiff objected to the request for admissions on grounds that they were signed by Daniel White when he was not an attorney of record. The court overrules this hypertechnical objection. The court finds that Mr. White signed the Requests for Admission on behalf of Dimitriou & Associates as essentially a contract attorney of that firm. The court also notes that Mr. White associated into the case shortly after the requests for admission were served and well before the responses were served. Plaintiff shall respond to all the Requests for Admission without making the overruled objection. Plaintiff also cannot try to avoid admitting or denying the requests by making up new objections now that their objection based on Mr. White not yet having associated into the case has been overruled. The amended responses shall be served by December 4, 2018.

The court reserves jurisdiction re compliance. Sanctions are denied unless plaintiff fails to serve responses to all requests for admission which comply with this Order by December 4, 2018. If defendants feel that plaintiff has not complied with this Order by December 4, 2018, they shall email the judge pro tem at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a cc to plaintiff to schedule a further hearing.

Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman will prepare an order.

Defendant Griffins Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Duncan MacDonald, Esq. of MacDonald Law, 415.956.7777, appearing for Defendant Susan Griffin;
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Susan Griffin's motion for relief from default is granted. The default entered against Ms. Griffin on January 23, 2018 is set aside and Ms. Griffin must file her answer no later than August 31, 2018. Ms. Griffin has shown sufficient grounds to set aside the default based on both the discretionary and mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Plaintiff Gilbert Papazian is estopped to assert that the portion of Ms. Griffin's motion seeking to set aside the default based on the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b) is untimely due to his counsel's filing of a notice of unavailability for the period July 3, 2018 through July 26, 2018. The court credits the statements of Ms. Griffin's counsel that but for the notice of unavailability and his desire to comply with it this motion would have been filed within 6 months of the entry of default. Where, as here, judgment has not been entered, there is no deadline for the filing of a motion based on the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b), so that part of the motion is timely even absent estoppel. Had Mr. Papazian and his counsel comported themselves in a manner consistent with the conduct expected of all litigants and their counsel, this motion would not have been required to be filed and thus no fees are awarded to Mr. Papazian in opposing the motion. The January 22, 2018 email of Mr. Papazian's counsel providing less than 24 hours' notice of his intent to seek the default of Ms. Griffin while knowing that Ms. Griffin's counsel may either be ill or on vacation falls well below the conduct expected of all counsel in civil litigation. The failure of Mr. Papazian to stipulate to setting aside the default as stated in the January 29, 2018 email of his counsel after learning that Ms. Griffin's counsel did not file an answer due to his illness and the illness of two of his children was yet further uncivil and unwarranted conduct. Order signed. (see order for full details)
